label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's think through this step by step. The question is asking whether following these instructions will bring me back to the starting point. The instructions are: 1. Take 3 steps. 2. Take 7 steps. 3. Turn around. 4. Take 10 steps. Let's assume I'm facing a particular direction (say, north) when I start. So initially, I take 3 steps north. Then I take 7 more steps north, so I've moved a total of 3 + 7 = 10 steps north from my starting point. Then I turn around, which means I'm facing south now. Then I take 10 steps, which would be 10 steps south. 10 steps south from my current position would bring me back to my starting point. Because I was 10 steps north of the start, and then I went 10 steps south, I end up back at the start. So following these instructions, I would return to the starting point. The answer is "Yes".
